$comment: Second profile-mode scope contract; first authorized Builder candidate. Seeded from profiles/expertmachina/scope-template.json (post-refresh), validated against ExpertMachina origin/main AFTER fetch. Predecessor (WS-finance-cost-leakage, SUPERSEDED-BY-EVENTS) is preserved in git history at a6582e7 and in its handoff records. All globs relative to targetRepo.
$commentValidated: origin/main @ 7c2203b (PR #30, release/v230-project-state, 2026-07-09), inspected after fetch. PR #30 was docs-only: workbench surface identical to the scout's 5fd448f baseline; constitutional tests present; roadmap NEXT is [OE] decision-minting — no next workbench planned by the target's own pipeline. Builder MUST re-fetch and re-verify this SHA at start; if origin/main has moved, STOP and return to Steward.
objective: Create the Operations / Process Improvement Workbench (canonical #8, Layer 3 greenfield in the ratified 16-workbench catalog) as the first clean AgenticTeam profile-mode Builder run: a Catalog v1 bundle at workbench/operations_process_improvement/ (workbench.yaml + five ratified ACTIVE skill contracts + synthetic corpus + acceptance runner) diagnosing process gaps, SOP inconsistencies, missing handoffs, and outdated procedure documents over governed process documents. It is a consumer OUTSIDE the governed backend using only existing doors, mirroring the newest shipped convention (finance_cost_leakage v2.3 as reference bundle), reusing workbench/common.py BY IMPORT only.
humanApproval: RATIFIED by Robert (robertkonecny@gmail.com) on 2026-07-09, in session: workbench #8 confirmed as first clean Builder candidate; five ACTIVE skills ratified with diagnostic/projection-only standing policy; profile-template refresh approved and folded into this pass. Ratification recorded in handoffs/2026-07-09-ws-operations-process-steward.md

skillContracts.$comment: Ratified 2026-07-09 from the target repo's own master skill registry (docs/workbench-skill-registry.md §8, [now]-tagged entries; assist per shipped 4+1 shape). The Builder implements exactly these five as skills/*.yaml declared contracts, no additions without re-scoping.

ratified.purpose: Finds contradictory instructions across process documents (doc-vs-doc conflict pattern).
Compares SOP content against governing policy documents on declared axes and flags mismatches.
Finds process steps whose handoff (next owner, trigger, or receiving procedure) is undefined in the documented process.
Detects process documents that appear superseded, stale, or misaligned with newer governed documents (revision-backed).
Assist: composes a cited, non-authoritative map of the documented process from governed evidence — a projection, never a source of truth.

skillContracts.excludedByDesign: Valve-synth contracts (generate_process_improvement_backlog, prepare_improvement_proposal), identify_missing_process_stage_owner [ES], and estimate_improvement_impact (SYNTHESIS_INFERRED) are out of scope for the first run; each requires machinery a first clean end-to-end run should not carry.
skillContracts.standingPolicy: This workbench is diagnostic/projection-only. It does not mutate canonical SOPs, policies, ownership records, workflow state, or audit state. Findings and suggested improvements re-enter only through the governed proposal lane.

preconditions: Builder fetches origin and verifies origin/main still equals validatedAgainst (7c2203b); if moved, STOP and return to Steward for re-validation
Builder starts from a fresh branch off up-to-date origin/main — never on top of a test/feature branch
All five ratified skill contracts must be authored as skills/*.yaml and ACTIVE per Catalog v1 convention before the runner will accept them
Builder verifies the proof-command invocation (venv path, pytest.ini discovery) on the baseline run BEFORE the first edit and reports any correction to the Steward rather than silently deviating
nonGoals: No changes to the governed backend (backend/app/**), MCP gateway, or backend/mcp_server.py
No edits to workbench/common.py or workbench/__init__.py — reuse common.py by import only (ruling-6 pattern)
No new fact approval states, evidence classes, or clearance levels
No vault writes; untouchable floor untouched
No valve-synth, [ES], or SYNTHESIS_INFERRED skills in this workstream (see skillContracts.excludedByDesign)
No [OE] dependencies: document-governed evidence only — no execution logs, traces, or operational-system records
No real company process documents in the corpus — synthetic SOPs/policies/checklists only
$commentAllowed: Test naming mirrors the newest shipped convention (test_finance_{corpus,acceptance,workbench}.py). The docs file mirrors docs/finance-cost-leakage-v2.3.md-style build records; exact title/versioning to match the target repo's docs conventions at build time.
$commentForbidden: Includes the refreshed six-shipped-workbench list, shared plumbing, and the target repo's constitutional/state documents (DECISIONS, catalog, registry, roadmap, PROJECT_STATE) — those change only through the target project's own process, never as a side effect of AgenticTeam work.
shippedWorkbenchCollisionPolicy: GREENFIELD-ONLY for this and any workstream until a shipped-workbench-extension policy is minted by its own Steward design pass. Shipped workbenches (#1,#2,#3,#5,#9,#16) and platform entries (#10,#13,#14,#15) are not build targets. If the Builder discovers mid-build that #8 territory overlaps a shipped surface in ANY way not visible at scoping, that is a BLOCKED finding to the Steward, not a judgment call.
projectionOnlyRule: The workbench consumes ONLY the existing doors: .empkg packages via the package channel and MCP queries at a real AGENT token's clearance. It never reads backend internals, the ledger, or raw stores directly. The assist skill (prepare_process_map_projection) produces a cited, visibly non-authoritative projection — never a source of truth. Data with no existing door = BLOCKED finding for the Steward.
canonicalMutationPolicy: Zero direct canonical writes — constitutional, not configurable (D29 One-Way Valve). Findings and improvement suggestions re-enter exclusively through the proposal lane (vault 08_proposals -> PROPOSAL-lane connector -> CANDIDATE -> human gate -> DERIVED fact). Proposal-lane candidates are never auto-approved. The permanent authorship guard must still prove from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts.
auditProvenanceExpectations: Every finding cites governed evidence with source, ingestion date, and approval state, following the shipped evidence-driven kinding pattern (refusal-first; declared same-subject/cross-document evidence rules in each skill contract; verbatim-or-declared derivations — the finance v2.3 'no invented values' discipline applies to process facts: no invented owners, dates, or process steps). Consumption flows through existing ledger mechanisms; this workstream adds no audit machinery and bypasses none.
permissionBoundaryExpectations: The runner operates at a real AGENT token's clearance; all reads are clearance-filtered by the existing engine. The acceptance test must include at least one denied-access case proving the workbench degrades to explicit refusal (not silent omission, not fabrication) when clearance excludes needed documents.
compileGateImpact: None permitted. backend/test_compile_gates.py and all permanent guards must pass unchanged. The new bundle must be structurally valid to the existing catalog convention without modifying any runner-side validation it is checked against. Zero new surface or law: no new decisions minted, no guard changes, common.py unchanged (a seventh time).

proofRequirements.mustShow: synthetic corpus documents load; five skill contracts parse and are ACTIVE; workbench.yaml is catalog-valid
each ratified skill produces evidence-cited findings on the seeded corpus; refusal-first on unsupported questions (profile rule 1)
explicit refusal/no-evidence output when the corpus lacks support — never fabricated findings; at least one refusal case per skill contract (profile rule 1)
the permanent authorship guard still proves from the ledger alone that no agent principal wrote canonical facts (profile rules 3-5, D29)
clearance filtering intact plus the workbench's denied-access case: explicit refusal under insufficient clearance (profile rule 7)
all compile gates pass unchanged (profile compile-gate neutrality)
no regressions vs a baseline run recorded BEFORE the first Builder edit (baseline output pasted in Builder handoff)

verificationGate.$comment: Per profiles/expertmachina/workflows/evidence-gate.md. A provenance or permission fail is never waivable by vote count — it escalates to a human.

rollbackAnchor: git tag ws-operations-process-baseline on the Builder's branch, created before the first edit
stopConditions: Builder verifies ALL preconditions first (incl. validatedAgainst re-check after fetch) and stops if any is unmet
Builder stops when all seven proofs have run with literal evidence and the handoff (incl. profile addendum A1-A5) is written; does not merge, does not push
Builder stops immediately if any needed data lacks an existing door, or if #8 territory unexpectedly overlaps a shipped surface (BLOCKED finding to Steward)
Sweeper stops if the baseline suite is red
Any agent stops immediately if work requires editing a forbiddenGlob
Any agent stops immediately upon finding a write path from workbench code to canonical state (critical defect, straight to human)

downstreamPlan.part: workbench bundle: workbench.yaml, five ratified ACTIVE skill contracts, synthetic corpus, acceptance runner (finance_cost_leakage v2.3 as newest-convention reference; common.py by import)
corpus + acceptance + workbench tests incl. refusal and denied-access cases

nextRecommendedAgent: builder / opus — authorized once this contract and the profile refresh are committed together (they are linked: the refresh is what makes this scope safe)
